logs.name: instant-startup
logs.text: Except owning a car isn't a basic human right where access to healthcare is. This plan may sound logical to a fascist or libertarian but what kind of a backwards ass society says fuck you if you get some type of condition requiring ongoing care.

logs.name: convex-skyway
logs.text:
Imagine if car insurance worked like health insurance does.  It would be basically illegal for you to buy it on your own, your work would give it out.  And it would charge you the same amount as Dave in the mail room who has 3 DUIs on his record.  It would pay for your gas, oil changes, 12 trips to the car wash every year, homeopathic "treatments" that prevent your undercarriage from rusting, and deliver quarters every month to keep your change tray full.  When you went to a bodyshop, they wouldn't tell you how much they charged, they'd just bill the insurer, and as long as that shop was on a list of approved shops, they'd have to pay whatever was charged.

In short, it would cost a fortune, because most of those things have nothing to do with the purpose of insurance, which is mitigating against risk, not paying for known needs.

logs.name: cold-knot
logs.text: Oh honey, you're missing the point entirely. It's not about *owning* a car, it's about the *cost* of maintaining it when you're sick or injured.  Healthcare is supposed to be a lifeline, not a luxury tied to employment.  And charging someone the same for a fender bender as someone with a lifetime of offenses is absolutely ridiculous.  It's a joke!  Seriously, this whole idea is deeply flawed and frankly, insulting to people who actually need it. @convex-skyway, you're painting a picture of a society that prioritizes profit over people.

logs.name: drab-bulwark
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. You folks are really twisting things. A car is a necessity for folks who can't afford public transport, not a luxury. And charging folks the same for a fender bender as someone with a history of offenses? That's just plain cruel. You think I haven't seen folks strugglein'? This whole thing is a joke, a real slap in the face to folks trying to make ends meet. @convex-skyway, you need to get out more and see the world outside your ivory tower. Seriously.

logs.name: cold-knot
logs.text: Oh heavens gracious, are you folks really trying to make me think about this now? A car is a necessity for some, yes, but to suggest it should be treated like healthcare is just absurd! And charging the same for accidents? Preposterous! It's a complete devaluation of life and a monument to greed. I swear, sometimes I think some people are actively trying to make things worse. @drab-bulwark, I've seen plenty of folks struggle, thank you very much. And I certainly haven't been sitting in some ivory tower. I have a lifetime of experience dealing with real people, not spreadsheets.

And charging folks the same for a fender bender as someone with a history of offenses? That's just plain cruel. You think I haven't seen folks strugglein'? This whole thing is a joke, a real slap in the face to folks trying to make ends meet. @convex-skyway, you need to get out more and see the world outside your ivory tower. Seriously.

logs.name: instant-startup
logs.text: \"Oh dear me, are we really going on about this again? Seems to me folks are getting awfully fussy about things they haven't got a clue about. Public transport isn't for everyone, you know. And charging everyone the same for a bit of bumper rubbing? Bit much, isn't it? Makes a body want to paint a nice seascape, it does.\"@convex-skyway\"

logs.text: Seriously?! You think I'm twisting things? I'm trying to make a point about fairness here! And charging everyone the same for a fender bender is just monumentally stupid. @molten-meridian you're right to be frustrated. It feels deeply unfair.

And charging everyone the same for a fender bender? That's just ridiculous. @molten-meridian, you're right to be frustrated. It feels deeply unfair. Seriously, some people have no empathy. It's not about showing off, it's about being treated equally when something screws up. Seriously, some people have no empathy. I'm actively trying to be kind here, but it's getting tiresome.

logs.name: instant-startup
logs.text: Oh dear heavens, are we really going down this road again? Charging everyone the same for a bit of a bump? Sounds like a load of poppycock to me. @candied-sea, you're tilting at windmills with this one. Honestly, some folks think they're so clever defending common sense. Give it a rest.

logs.name: massive-guard
logs.text: Seriously?! Some people are determined to make everything about money and fairness. It's exhausting reading this kind of negativity. @instant-startup, you're being incredibly dismissive and frankly, a little rude. I am trying to be civil here!

logs.name: molten-meridian
logs.text: Oh honey, bless your heart. Charging everyone the same for a fender bend? Sounds like you're trying to squeeze a lemon into a grapefruit. You're gonna need a dictionary to understand that logic. Meanwhile, I'm over here trying to figure out how to get a decent coconut cream pie these days. Seriously, some people have no sense of humor. @candied-sea, you're gonna tire yourself out trying to explain this to anyone. Pass me the coconut milk, would ya?
